content: Calibration-driven quantization: GPTQ uses calibration data + second-order (Hessian) info, quantizes each weight column to minimize reconstruction error while compensating downstream columns; 3-4 bits, few GPU-hours, mild overfitting risk. AWQ collects activation statistics offline, finds ~1% salient weight channels, applies per-channel scaling (absorbed into preceding layer via equivalent transform) so salient weights are protected without mixed-precision; no backprop, generalizes, 4-bit primary (3-bit kernels limited). llama.cpp uses --imatrix (importance matrix from calibration set) to enhance GGUF quant quality, esp at <4 bit. transformers supports AwqConfig/GPTQConfig with calib dataset. All serious tools require/optionally use calibration.
